sutta: once the exalted 1 was dwelling in ambapali's grove near stop here for a moment this is mentioned in the samadhi and vinaya he was she was a very beautiful courtesan cortisone is a nice sounding word it is it means a high-class prostitute in the days of the buddha learning or living as a prostitute was not really considered wrong and she was very beautiful and she gave a piece of land to the buddha to make into a monastery and later in life she renounced and became a nun and in the theory versus of the arahanza she described her life and she described how when she was young she was so beautiful and the hair was jet black and curly and she described the skin was white and soft and all that now all the different parts of the body and then when she was older she described how the body has become and the way she described was quite moving and how she became an arahant and all that so that's then and the suta continues there the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying monks lord they replied and the exalted 1 said impermanent monks are conditioners sankara unstable monks are conditioners insecure monks are conditioners so monks be you dissatisfied with all things of this world be you repelled by them be you utterly free from them let's stop here for a moment this word sankara here we translate as conditioners some other books they translate as conditioned things but the actual word for conditioned things is we prefer to translate it as conditioners conditioners are those things that condition something else but anything in the world everything in the world are conditioners in the sense that they condition other things but they are also conditioned things because they depend on other conditions so conditioners are also in a way condition things but the reason the buddha we feel like use it in the sense of conditioners is because in some other suta the buddha said that all conditioners are impermanent if everything that conditions say for example your body or your mind are impermanent then if those things that condition you are impermanent then all the more are you impermanent just like for example a shadow of a tree a shadow of a tree depends on the tree but the tree is impermanent therefore the shadow must be even more impermanent so there's a difference now the buddha continued monks this king of mountains is 84 000 leaks in length 84 000 leaks in breath 84 000 leaks immersed in the great ocean it stands out above the waters of the ocean 84 000 leaks monks there comes a time when for many years for many hundreds of years for many thousands of years for many hundreds of thousands of years there is no rain and when the rains come not all seed life and vegetation all trees that yield medicine palms and giants of the jungle become parched and dried up and are no more thus impermanent thus unstable thus insecure are all conditioners be you dissatisfied with them be you repelled by them are you utterly free from them just stop here for a moment in some other suta the buddha mentioned that life depends on water in other words life depends on rain all life on our planet is sustained by rain if there were no rain then life forms slowly will disappear so here is mentioned now when there's no rain for thousands and thousands of years then all the plants all die and when the plants die other life that depends on the plants also will die and this this fact that the buddha said that life depends on water now science begins to realize this that's why when they send these satellites to other planets they try to find out whether there's any water on the other planets because now scientists and they realize if there's any water at all in the other planets that means there must have been life there now the buddha continues monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of some vast period a second sun appears when the second sun appears all the streams and the pawns become past and dried up and are no more thus impermanent thus unstable thus in secure all conditioners be you utterly free from them monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of some vast period a third sun appears when the third sun appears all the great rivers become parched and dried and dried up and are no more that is to say the ganges the yamuna the achiravati the sarabu and the mahi thus impermanent thus unstable thus insecure all conditioners be you utterly free from them monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of sambar's period a fourth sun appears when the fourth sun appears all the great lakes from where these great rivers flow become past and dried up and are no more that is to say the rata the kana munda the kunala the chadanta thus impermanent thus unstable thus insecure are all conditioners be you utterly free from them monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of sambar's period a fifth son appears when the fifth sun appears the waters of the mighty ocean recede a 100 leaks the waters recede 200 leaks 3 4 5 6 and 7 100 leaves the waters of the mighty ocean remain at a depth of 7 palm trees of 6 5 4 3 2 of nearly 1 palm tree the waters of the mighty ocean remain at a depth of 7 men's station of height of 6 5 4 3 2 of merely 1 man's height of just half a man of merely up to a man's hip of merely up to his knee of merely up to his ankle monks just as in the autumn time when the rain deva sheds big drops of rain here and there in the footprints of cows there are puddles even so monks as mere puddles in the in a cow's footprints are the waters of the mighty ocean here and there thus impermanent thus unstable thus insecure are all conditioners be you utterly free from them monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of sambar's period a sick sun appears when the sixth sun appears both this earth and simaru king of mountains emit smoke disgorged smoke belch fought clouds or smoke monks just as a potter's oven when first lightened emits smoke disgorges smoke belches forth clouds or smoke even so monks when the sixth sun appears both this great earth and simaru king of mountains emit smoke discard smoke and belch forth clouds or smoke thus impermanent thus unstable thus insecure are all conditioners be you utterly free from them monks there comes a time when in some age at the end of sambar's period a seventh son appears when the seventh son appears this great earth and simaru king of mountains bursts into flames blaze up and become a single sheet of flame and the fiery beam of the flame of the blaze and the burn of the great earth and of mount shimeru thrown up by the winds reaches even to brahma's world the peaks of mount simaru measuring 1 2 3 4 and 5 100 leaks as it blazes and burns vanquished and overwhelmed by the vastness of the fiery mass crumbled away out of the blaze and the burn of the great earth and mount simeru there is neither cinder nor ash to be found monks just as out of blazing burning ghee or oil no cinder or ash is found even so monks out of the blaze and burn of the great earth and mount simaru neither cinder nor ash is to be found thus impermanent monks are conditioners thus unstable monks are conditioners thus insecure among us are conditioners wherefore monks be you dissatisfied with all things of this world be you repelled by them be you utterly free from them i'll just stop here for a moment you see this is very interesting it describes very clearly how the world is going to be destroyed in the future and this is the only suta in the nikayas which has this description you know of how the world is going to be destroyed by fire so when people talk about the end of the world we don't have to worry because the second sun has not appeared the rains must stop the rains must stop for thousands and thousands of years and then when that happens then slowly here the the the trees and start to die off and then after a long time the second sun appears and when the second sun appears and the streams and the pawns dry up and then after a long time a third son appears and when the third sun appears the big reversal the ganges etc also will dry up and then after a long time a fourth sun appears and when the fourth sun appears the great lakes behind even those behind the [Music] himalayas they will dry up and then after a long time a fifth sun appears and when the fifth sun appears the waters of the ocean begin to dry up and dry up until they become just puddles of water here and there and then when the sick sun appears and the earth starts to emit smoke a lot of smoke starts to come up and then when the seventh sun appears and the earth will start to burn and burn up to the heavens and burn up to the brahma's world so this is very interesting because other religions also talk about the fact that the world be destroyed by fire but it won't give such a clear description as the buddha now the buddha continue now where is the sage where is the believer who thinks this great earth and mount simuru will be burnt up will utterly perish and be no more save or accept among those who have seen the goal in days gone by monks there was a teacher named sunita a core setter who was free from all lustful passions now he had many hundreds of disciples to whom he taught the doctrine of fellowship in brahma's world and all they who grafts the word taught by sunita in its fullness on the breaking up of the body after death were reborn in the realm of bliss the brahma world but of those who did not grasp the word in full some after death were reborn into the fellowship of the devas who had who have power over others creations some among the devas who delight in creating some among the tusita devas sung among the yama devas some among the devas of the 33. some in the company of the 4 royal devas some among wealthy nobles some among wealthy brahmins and some among wealthy householders now sunita the teacher taught thus it is not seemly that i should have precisely the same mode of existence as my disciples after death what if i were to make metta of goodwill become to a perfect degree and sunita the teacher cultivated the thought of mehta for 7 years and then for 7 world cycles returned not to this world he arose in the sphere of radiance that means in the jana plains in brahma's fear as maha brahma asaka devaraja as a real turning king endowed with the 7 gems yet yet monks although sunita lived so long and lasted such a time he was not freed from birth from ageing from dying from sorrow lamentation pain grief and despair he was not freed from dukkha i say and what is the reason it was by not being awake too by not penetrating 4 conditions what for monks it was by not being away by not being awake too by not penetrating aryan right conduct it was by not being awake too by not penetrating aryan concentration it was by not being awake too by not penetrating aryan wisdom and it was by not being awake to by not penetrating aryan release monks it is justice when aryan right conduct is awakened and penetrated when aryan when aryan concentration is awakened and penetrated when aryan wisdom is awakened and penetrated and when aryan release is awakened and penetrated the craving for life is cut off the court that binds 1 to becoming is destroyed and there is no more coming to be that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so on this last part man the buddha is talking about sunita of course that of course settle means 1 of those religious leaders who start a new segment in chinese i think it's called kai kai tao 1 who starts a new religion and it's mentioned here that he was free from all lustful passions free from all logical passions even though this man sunita he had not become an arya yet the suta says that he was freed from all lustful passions and this shows that this man because he cultivated the janas he cultivated the brahma viharas to attain rebirth in the brahma world so he had attained the janus so just by attaining jana's 1 becomes freed of all lustful passions that means the hindrances are abandoned this is 1 of the sutasa that confirms that when 1 attains the jaanas the hindrances are abandoned that means cut down although the roots are not pulled up by yet now it's just like a tall grass you cut down the tall grass completely even though the roots are not pulled up now the other thing mentioned here is that and all they who grasp the word taught by soneta in its fullness on the breaking up of the body after death were reborn in the realm of bliss the brahma world but of those who did not grasp the word in full some after death were reborn into the fellowship of the devas who have power over others creation some among the devas would delight in creating some among the tusita devas etc etc the lower devasa so here it shows that grasping the word of the teacher is very important those who grasp the word of the teacher then they could reborn into the highest state so in the same way the word of the buddha is very important to us it is very very important to understand the buddha's words very very well if you understand the su then you will get the 100 of the result of the buddha's teachings if you don't understand the word of the buddha then we would get nothing out of being a buddhist so it is very important to listen to the discourses of the buddha and understand them in the last part of the sutta the buddha said it is by cultivating 3 things are in right conduct aryan concentration aryan wisdom and iron release that a person um ends the round of de-birds and destroys dukkha uh suffering actually the the last part i don't release is the result of attaining the first 3 so it is the first 3 that's important silas ahmadi and panya moral conduct concentration and wisdom and these 3 things are also the noble eightfold path or the aryan eightfold path taught by the buddha so if we cultivate the uh the rna4 path or this sila samadhi and panya fully then we attain a full awakening full liberation
